task: I’m trying to decide whether to buy a used truck I found, but I don’t want to jump in and regret it later. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist for what I should verify before I spend the money?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **go / no-go used truck checklist** you can use before buying.

## Used Truck Go / No-Go Checklist

### 1) Identity / paperwork
- [ ] Title is clean and matches seller
- [ ] VIN on title matches VIN on truck
- [ ] No salvage, flood, rebuilt, or lemon branding unless you’re okay with that
- [ ] Mileage makes sense with wear and records
- [ ] Maintenance records available
- [ ] No open recalls that matter to you

**No-go if:** title problems, VIN mismatch, branded title you don’t want, or missing paperwork that can’t be explained.

---

### 2) Quick visual walkaround
- [ ] Body panels line up reasonably
- [ ] No obvious fresh paint hiding repairs
- [ ] No rust-through on frame, cab mounts, rocker panels, or bed supports
- [ ] No signs of flood damage: mud, silt, water lines, musty smell
- [ ] Tires wear evenly
- [ ] Glass, lights, mirrors all intact

**No-go if:** frame rust, flood signs, or major crash repair.

---

### 3) Under-hood check
- [ ] Fluids are clean enough and at proper levels
- [ ] No coolant/oil/fuel leaks
- [ ] Belts and hoses look serviceable
- [ ] Battery terminals are clean and tight
- [ ] Grounds look intact
- [ ] No hacked wiring, tape jobs, or obvious electrical damage

**No-go if:** major leaks, overheating signs, or electrical hack work.

---

### 4) Start-up and idle
- [ ] Starts quickly
- [ ] Idles smoothly
- [ ] No excessive smoke from exhaust
- [ ] No warning lights that stay on
- [ ] No knocking, ticking, or loud belt noise

**No-go if:** hard start, rough idle, heavy smoke, or engine noise.

---

### 5) Scan tool check
If you can, plug in an OBD2 scanner:
- [ ] No stored or pending codes
- [ ] Readiness monitors look normal
- [ ] Fuel trims aren’t way out of range
- [ ] Coolant temp rises normally
- [ ] Misfire counts are zero or very low

**No-go if:** lots of codes, active misfires, or weird live data.

---

### 6) Test drive
- [ ] Transmission shifts smoothly
- [ ] No slipping, harsh shifts, or delayed engagement
- [ ] Steering tracks straight
- [ ] Brakes don’t pull or pulsate badly
- [ ] 4WD/AWD engages if equipped
- [ ] No vibration under acceleration or at highway speed
- [ ] No overheating during drive

**No-go if:** transmission problems, overheating, driveline vibration, or brake issues.

---

### 7) Chassis / suspension
- [ ] Ball joints, tie rods, bushings, shocks look okay
- [ ] No clunks over bumps
- [ ] No excessive play in steering
- [ ] Frame is straight and not heavily rusted

**No-go if:** loose steering, major suspension wear, or structural rust.

---

### 8) Final decision triggers
**Walk away if you find:**
- Title or VIN issues
- Frame rust or flood damage
- Overheating
- Transmission slipping
- Major engine noise
- Heavy smoke
- Electrical gremlins
- Evidence of a bad crash repair

---

## Simple rule
**Go** if it passes paperwork, rust/flood check, starts clean, drives well, and scans clean.  
**No-go** if it has any structural, title, overheating, or transmission red flags.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** or a **more detailed truck-specific version** you can take with you when you inspect it.

user_simulator.text:
Yep. That’s solid. Clean, practical, no fluff. I’d use that as a starting point.

Only thing I’d add is a hard line on **service history** and **cold start**:
- [ ] Cold start only, not warmed up before you get there
- [ ] Proof of oil changes, trans service, coolant work if they claim it was done

And I’d make this a no-go if the seller won’t let you take it for a real drive or won’t let a shop inspect it. That’s usually where the problems show up.

Otherwise, that checklist works.
